query: 2026 Florida Republican gubernatorial primary status: Byron Donalds candidacy, opponents, Trump endorsement, polling, competitors dropping out. Who is running against Donalds and what are the polls showing as of mid-2026?
answer:
I've completed the research. Here's a summary of the key findings on the 2026 Florida Republican gubernatorial primary as of mid-July 2026:

**Byron Donalds is the overwhelming frontrunner:**
- Trump-endorsed early and fully (Feb 2025), officially qualified for the ballot
- Now consolidating the establishment—DeSantis allies including AG James Uthmeier endorsed him in early July 2026, though Gov. DeSantis himself still won't endorse anyone

**Who's running against him:**
- **Jay Collins** (DeSantis-appointed Lt. Gov., Green Beret) — the clear #2, entered Jan 2026
- **James Fishback** (investor) — third
- **Paul Renner** (ex-House Speaker) — trailing badly in low single digits
- Plus several minor candidates

**Casey DeSantis** was heavily floated and polled hypothetically but **never entered** the race. **No major candidate has dropped out**—only minor figures withdrew.

**Polling:** Donalds leads by huge margins in public polls (46% Emerson, 54% AIF, 51% 270toWin average). The one outlier showing a tighter race (Donalds 37% vs. Collins 20%) is a **Collins campaign internal poll**, so it warrants skepticism. Polymarket priced Donalds at ~95.8% to win the primary as of July 12, 2026.

The full report and structured data (with all sources and quantitative data points) have been delivered.

sources.title: Byron Donalds' chances of beating Casey DeSantis in Florida—polls

key_facts: The 2026 Florida GOP gubernatorial primary is scheduled for August 18, 2026; general election November 3, 2026. Ron DeSantis is term-limited.
Byron Donalds (R-Naples) is the clear frontrunner, endorsed early and fully by President Trump (Feb 2025), and has officially qualified for the ballot.
In early July 2026, DeSantis allies including AG James Uthmeier endorsed Donalds; Gov. DeSantis himself has still not endorsed anyone.
Main active opponents: Lt. Gov. Jay Collins (entered Jan 12, 2026), investor James Fishback, and former House Speaker Paul Renner.
Casey DeSantis was widely floated and polled hypothetically but never entered the race and did not qualify.
No major candidate has dropped out; only minor candidates withdrew or were disqualified (Curtman, Mercadante, Cruel, Andros, Imperato).
Emerson College poll (Mar 29-31, 2026): Donalds 46%, Collins 4%, Fishback 4%, 39% undecided; with Casey DeSantis included Donalds 44% vs DeSantis 7%.
Associated Industries of Florida poll (June 3-7, 2026): Donalds 54%, leading nearest rival by 46 points, Fishback 8%.
A Jay Collins campaign internal poll (early July 2026) showed a tighter race: Donalds ~37%, Collins ~20%, Fishback ~9.6%, Renner ~2.1%.
Polymarket priced Donalds to win the GOP primary at ~95.8% as of July 12, 2026 (range 95.6-96.5% over July 5-12).
